What Is Env and When Should You Use It?

A quick rundown of what .env is and when it makes sense to use one. Covers what env vars are for, the common file conventions (.env.development, .env.production, .env.example), how to load them with dotenv, when to use env vars beyond secrets, and the critical caveat that frontend env vars are NOT a place to store sensitive info — they end up in your built bundle.
Expose a Local Server to the Internet With ngrok (Windows + macOS Setup)

ngrok is a handy way to expose a local dev server to the internet — roughly 5 minutes of setup gets you a public HTTPS URL that forwards to your localhost. The first-run setup has a few quirks (especially on macOS — Gatekeeper blocks the binary; PATH needs configuring), so this post walks through Windows + macOS setup end to end. Useful for sharing a local prototype, testing webhooks, or wiring up a Line Bot without deploying.
Gmail Hidden Tips You Probably Don't Know

Most of us use Gmail to sign up for services — GitHub, AWS, Heroku, and so on. This post covers two underused Gmail features that make signup and sending safer: the plus-suffix alias trick (yourname+something@gmail.com) for managing per-service signups and spotting leaks, and Gmail's Undo Send delay so you can claw a badly-sent email back.
Homebrew zsh: command not found: brew — How I Fixed It

One day brew install just stopped working — zsh told me it couldn't find "brew install", but plain `brew` did show output. This post walks through what was wrong (PATH-related setup against the wrong shell rc file, plus a missing wget dependency), and the steps that actually got brew install back up and running.
Deploy Your Node.js Project to fly.io With flyctl

A walkthrough of deploying an Express app to fly.io. After Heroku killed its free tier, fly.io became one of the alternatives worth trying. This post covers installing flyctl on macOS/Windows/Linux, registering, signing in, deploying with flyctl launch, and checking deploy status. Heads up — fly.io now requires a credit card on file even for the free tier.
Migrating From Heroku to Render: A Deployment Guide

Heroku was my go-to backend platform for years, but in 2022/11/28 they killed the free tier. This post is the walkthrough I went through migrating my sites to Render.com — why I picked Render, how to deploy a Node.js project end to end (with environment variables), and the Render-specific quirks worth knowing about (sleep mode, shared monthly hours, slow free-tier deploys, the HTTP endpoint requirement, and bots that send twice).
Buy a Domain (Google Domains) and Point It at Your Blog on GitHub Pages With Cloudflare

Heads up — Google Domains was discontinued in 2023 and transferred to Squarespace. The Google Domains-specific parts of this post are historical, but the GitHub Pages + Cloudflare setup is still applicable. If you're setting up a new domain today, see my newer post on Cloudflare Domain instead. Keeping this post as historical reference for anyone who already has a Google Domain.
You Don't Have to Read Clean Code to Write Decent Code

This post took me close to a year to write — it's a collection of real code smells I've seen in my time as a TA, anonymized and cleaned up. Not a Clean Code book recap; more like "here are common patterns that hurt readability, and how to write them so future-you (or your reviewer) doesn't suffer." Naming, var vs let/const, ES6 destructuring, comments, formatting, ternaries, loops, and not mixing Composition API with Options API.
Teachable Video Shows Blank? Browser Strict Privacy Settings Are the Likely Cause

Some readers told me Teachable videos suddenly stopped playing, but my own browser was fine. Turned out the culprit was their browser's Strict privacy mode. This post walks through the fix in Firefox, Chrome, and Edge, plus the fallback when toggling privacy mode isn't enough.
Vite Deployed to GitHub Pages but _plugin-vue_export-helper Returns 404

Vite builds and ships to GitHub Pages fine — but once you load the page, the dynamically imported _plugin-vue_export-helper module 404s and the page goes blank. This post walks through the actual cause (Rollup's filename sanitization producing a name GitHub Pages doesn't serve) and the vite.config.js tweak that fixes it.
